qa_pairs.question: What are the start and deadline dates for Resources for Research Groups (RRG) and Research Platforms and Portals (RPP) applications?
qa_pairs.answer: Applications for both RRG and RPP start on September 23, 2025, with a deadline of November 4, 2025, at 11:59 p.m. Eastern Standard Time.

qa_pairs.question: Is it possible to get an extension for the application deadline?
qa_pairs.answer: No, an extension of the November 4, 2025, 11:59 p.m. Eastern Standard Time deadline is not possible.

qa_pairs.question: What is the primary purpose of the Resource Allocation Competition (RAC)?
qa_pairs.answer: The RAC enables faculty members and their research groups to access compute, storage, and cloud resources beyond what is available through the Rapid Access Service (RAS).

qa_pairs.question: Who oversees the Resource Allocation Competition (RAC)?
qa_pairs.answer: The RAC is overseen by the Resource Access Program Administrative Committee (RAPAC), which includes representatives from all regional partners and national system host sites.

qa_pairs.question: What should applicants requesting GPU and/or cloud resources do?
qa_pairs.answer: They should attend one of the corresponding information sessions.

qa_pairs.question: What is strongly encouraged for first-time RAC applicants?
qa_pairs.answer: First-time applicants are strongly encouraged to schedule a pre-submission consultation before October 31.

qa_pairs.question: What is a critical requirement regarding the application template?
qa_pairs.answer: Applicants must use the most up-to-date version of the application template provided in the guide, as failing to do so may result in rejection.

qa_pairs.question: Where can applicants find the evaluation criteria for RAC applications?
qa_pairs.answer: The evaluation criteria are available in Appendix A for Resources for Research Groups and Appendix B for Research Platforms and Portals of the document.

qa_pairs.question: What is the primary purpose of the Resources for Research Groups (RRG) application process?
qa_pairs.answer: The RRG process is for projects whose primary purpose is to conduct research requiring compute, storage, and cloud resources to meet their goals.

qa_pairs.question: When should projects apply through the RPP process instead of RRG?
qa_pairs.answer: Projects primarily needing persistent instances in the cloud to provide a service through a platform or portal should apply through the RPP application process.

qa_pairs.question: What is the RRG Fast Track application process?
qa_pairs.answer: Users with an existing RRG award who meet eligibility criteria can submit a lightweight progress report to renew their request for computational resources, instead of a new application.

qa_pairs.question: What is the primary purpose of the Research Platforms and Portals (RPP) application process?
qa_pairs.answer: The RPP process is for projects whose primary purpose is to provide a service through scientific gateways that improve access to shared datasets, enhance online research tools, or advance national/international research collaborations.

qa_pairs.question: What is the definition of a research platform or portal in this context?
qa_pairs.answer: A research platform or portal is a set of community-developed tools, applications, and data integrated via a gateway or suite of applications (usually with a graphical user interface) customized for a specific discipline's user community.

qa_pairs.question: What are the two main requirements for projects applying through RPP?
qa_pairs.answer: Projects must provide a service to a larger research community via cloud-based tools/data and be able to develop, operate, and manage the proposed platform/portal with minimal support from the Federation.

qa_pairs.question: What must holders of multi-year RPP awards expiring in 2026 do to continue their resource needs?
qa_pairs.answer: They must submit a new RPP application in the fall if their need for resources will continue for the 2026-2027 allocation cycle.

qa_pairs.question: Are multi-year RPP projects required to submit a new application every year?
qa_pairs.answer: No, multi-year projects do not need to submit a new application annually but are required to complete an annual progress report.

qa_pairs.question: Who is eligible to submit a RAC application?
qa_pairs.answer: The Principal Investigator (PI) and all Co-PIs must be faculty members at a Canadian academic institution and have an active Alliance account in the CCDB with an Academic PI role (Faculty, Adjunct Faculty, or Librarian).

qa_pairs.question: What are the limitations for PIs regarding submitting RRG and RPP applications?
qa_pairs.answer: A PI can apply for only one RRG application at a time (full or Fast Track) but can be a Co-PI or collaborator on others; they can also be the PI for one RPP application per competition round and a Co-PI on other RPP applications.

qa_pairs.question: Are 'Departmental' applications accepted by the RAC?
qa_pairs.answer: No, 'Departmental' applications, submitted on behalf of a group of PIs not collaborating on a common research project with clear goals, will not be accepted.

qa_pairs.question: Who is considered a Co-PI in this competition?
qa_pairs.answer: A Co-PI is any Canadian faculty member with an Alliance Academic PI role who is actively involved in the computational project.

qa_pairs.question: What is required from the PI and any Co-PIs with the application?
qa_pairs.answer: The PI and all Co-PIs must upload an updated Canadian Common CV (CCV) with the application, and the role and involvement of any listed Co-PI must be justified.

qa_pairs.question: When should applicants not submit a RAC application for computational resources?
qa_pairs.answer: If their need for computational resources can be met with what is available via the Rapid Access Service (RAS), they should not submit a RAC application.

qa_pairs.question: Which storage types are not allocated through the RAC?
qa_pairs.answer: /HOME storage and /SCRATCH storage are not allocated through the RAC.

qa_pairs.question: What is the minimum CPU request eligible for an HPC RAC application?
qa_pairs.answer: A CPU request must be equal to or greater than 200 core-years or core-equivalent-years in each cluster where resources are needed.

qa_pairs.question: How are core-equivalent-years calculated for high memory per core requests?
qa_pairs.answer: Core-equivalent-years are calculated as MAX(cores, mem requested / 4GB), where Mem requested = CY * mem per core requested. The resulting calculation must be greater than 200 for eligibility.

qa_pairs.question: What is the minimum GPU request eligible for an HPC RAC application?
qa_pairs.answer: A GPU request must be equal to or greater than 25 reference GPU units (RGU)-years in each cluster where resources are needed.

qa_pairs.question: What is an RGU (Reference GPU Unit)?
qa_pairs.answer: An RGU is a unit measuring the amount of GPU resources used, representing the 'cost' of utilizing a particular GPU model, whose value varies based on performance.

qa_pairs.question: What is the minimum Project storage (TB) eligible for an HPC RAC application?
qa_pairs.answer: The minimum is 41 TB, which is the sum of all project storage needed across all General Purpose clusters (Fir, Nibi, Rorqual, Narval).

qa_pairs.question: What is the minimum Nearline storage (TB) eligible for an HPC RAC application?
qa_pairs.answer: The minimum is 101 TB, which is the sum of all nearline storage needed across all General Purpose clusters (Fir, Nibi, Rorqual, Narval).

qa_pairs.question: Is there a minimum amount for dCache storage (TB) for a RAC application?
qa_pairs.answer: No, any amount of dCache storage must be requested through RAC.

qa_pairs.question: What are the minimums for Cloud Compute Cloud VCPU, Compute Cloud VGPU, Persistent Cloud VCPU, Volume and Snapshot Storage, Shared Filesystem Storage, and Object Storage?
qa_pairs.answer: The minimums are 81 VCPU-years, 3.6 RGU-years, 26 VCPU-years, 11 TB, 11 TB, and 11 TB, respectively. Only one of these resources needs to meet its minimum in a particular cloud site for the request to be eligible.

qa_pairs.question: What happens if there are discrepancies between the online application form and the attached Application Document?
qa_pairs.answer: In case of discrepancy, what was requested in the online form will prevail.

qa_pairs.question: What is the maximum page limit for RAC applications before references?
qa_pairs.answer: Applications exceeding 10 pages before references will not be accepted.

qa_pairs.question: What are the submission requirements for the application document regarding format and references?
qa_pairs.answer: The document must be submitted in PDF format. References can be a separate document if the total application (including references) exceeds 10 pages; otherwise, everything can be in one document.

qa_pairs.question: Where must RAC proposals be submitted?
qa_pairs.answer: Proposals must be submitted electronically through the CCDB portal.

qa_pairs.question: What is the deadline for submitting RAC proposals?
qa_pairs.answer: The deadline is November 4, 2025, at 11:59 p.m. Eastern Standard Time.

qa_pairs.question: When will applicants be informed about the status of their applications?
qa_pairs.answer: Applicants will be informed via email in spring 2026.

qa_pairs.question: What is the goal of pre-submission consultations?
qa_pairs.answer: The goal is to determine if resources needed justify a RAC application, verify project eligibility for RPP vs. RRG, and provide technical assistance with resource calculation.

qa_pairs.question: By when should pre-submission consultations be scheduled?
qa_pairs.answer: Consultations should happen prior to October 31, 2025.

qa_pairs.question: What is required for the peer review process regarding the Canadian Common CV (CCV)?
qa_pairs.answer: An up-to-date Canadian Common CV (CCV) is required for the peer review process for both the PI and all Co-PIs.

qa_pairs.question: What is the consequence of failing to provide an updated CCV?
qa_pairs.answer: Failing to provide an updated CCV will negatively impact the overall score of the submitted application.

qa_pairs.question: Can deadline extensions be granted due to missing CCVs?
qa_pairs.answer: No, deadline extensions due to missing CCVs are not possible.

qa_pairs.question: What are the guiding principles of the RAC assessment process?
qa_pairs.answer: All applications receive fair consideration through peer and technical review, resources are awarded based on appropriateness and efficient use, and challenges from resource shortages are shared among applicants.

qa_pairs.question: Who conducts the technical review in the RAC assessment process?
qa_pairs.answer: Technical experts of the Federation conduct the technical review.

qa_pairs.question: What is the expected response time for applicants to requests from the technical team during the review process?
qa_pairs.answer: Applicants are expected to respond to requests from the technical team within 48 hours.

qa_pairs.question: What is the peer review score based on?
qa_pairs.answer: The score is based on the feasibility of the computational research project, the appropriateness of requested resources, and the likelihood of efficient resource use.

qa_pairs.question: How are requests for allocations scaled?
qa_pairs.answer: Requests for allocations are scaled every year based on the overall score of the application and the supply and demand for resources.

qa_pairs.question: What are the two evaluation criteria for Resources for Research Groups (RRG) applications?
qa_pairs.answer: The two criteria are 'Research Methods' (70%) and 'Resource Management and Computational Expertise' (30%).

qa_pairs.question: What does the 'Research Methods' criterion evaluate for RRG applications?
qa_pairs.answer: This criterion evaluates the proposed methods to achieve computational project objectives, the appropriateness of requested resources, and the technical justification provided.

qa_pairs.question: What does the 'Resource Management and Computational Expertise' criterion evaluate for RRG applications?
qa_pairs.answer: This criterion evaluates the research team's capacity to manage the project, efficiently use requested resources, and the overall feasibility of the project based on the team's expertise.

qa_pairs.question: What are the two evaluation criteria for Research Platforms and Portals (RPP) applications?
qa_pairs.answer: The two criteria are 'Project Justification' (50%) and 'Resource Management and Expertise of the Team' (50%).

qa_pairs.question: What does the 'Project Justification' criterion evaluate for RPP applications?
qa_pairs.answer: This criterion evaluates the problem/need addressed, objectives/goals, added value, use of the platform/portal, expected outcomes, progress over the past year, and justification of the resource request.

qa_pairs.question: What does the 'Resource Management and Expertise of the Team' criterion evaluate for RPP applications?
qa_pairs.answer: This criterion evaluates the team's capacity to manage, develop, and operate the platform/portal, their ability to efficiently use resources, and the overall project feasibility based on their expertise.

qa_pairs.question: What is the scoring scale for RAC applications and what score is considered unsuccessful?
qa_pairs.answer: RAC applications are scored on a 5-point scale (0.1 to 5.0). Applications with a score of 2.0 or lower are considered unsuccessful and will not be awarded.

qa_pairs.question: What is required of peer reviewers before accessing any RAC application?
qa_pairs.answer: Peer reviewers are required to sign a Non-Disclosure Agreement and Conflict of Interest Policy.

qa_pairs.question: When is a reviewer in 'direct conflict' with a RAC application?
qa_pairs.answer: A reviewer is in direct conflict if they submitted an application to the same committee, are from the same university department, were a supervisor/student within 5 years, collaborated/published within 2 years (or plan to), provide direct/indirect support, are a relative/close friend, had public scientific/personal differences, or stand to gain/lose financially.

qa_pairs.question: What personal information collected by the Alliance is used for?
qa_pairs.answer: Any personal information collected by the Alliance is used only to review applications and may be shared with relevant officials in regional partners and research institutions.

qa_pairs.question: What information about successful applications will the Alliance make public?
qa_pairs.answer: The Alliance will make public the PI name, institution, department, research area, project title, project summary, summary of resources awarded, and application process for successful applications.
